reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement. `D = ball 0 1` is the open unit disc, `DifferentiableOn ℂ f D` means f is holomorphic on D, `ContinuousOn f (closure D)` captures the continuous extension of f to the closed disc (which includes the boundary `sphere 0 1 = ∂D`), and `sphere (0:ℂ) 1` is exactly the boundary of the unit disc. The conclusion `¬ ∀ z ∈ sphere 0 1, f z = 1/z` asserts for any such f that the boundary equation f(z) = 1/z cannot hold everywhere on ∂D, which is logically equivalent to "there is no holomorphic function extending continuously to ∂D with f(z) = 1/z on ∂D". The universally quantified structure over f with the conclusion being the negation of the boundary condition correctly encodes the non-existence claim.
